summary: Russian forces used a fiber-optic FPV drone "Barmaley" to destroy a Ukrainian MaxxPro armored vehicle in Dobropole, operated by the 56th Separate Airborne Assault Brigade. Ukrainian FPV pilots are targeting Russian forces on horseback, while Russian operators of the Rubicon Center reported 104 strikes against Ukrainian positions on March 11, 2026, including FPV air defense against Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs. A Russian infantryman attempted to charge a Ukrainian FPV drone with a bayonet and was killed. Ukrainian forces are deploying FPV detectors to counter enemy drones, and there is a noted increase in Russian "Molnia" strike and reconnaissance drones, which are difficult to counter due to their one-way signal control. Additionally, Russian forces used the "Yolka" air defense drone for kinetic intercepts against Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs in spring 2026.
